title: beyeria viscosa var. oblongifolia
intro: Beyeria viscosa var. oblongifolia, also known as Sticky Beyeria, is a plant species native to Western Australia, characterized by its sticky resinous branches and small white flowers. Traditionally used in Australian herbal medicine, Beyeria viscosa var. oblongifolia has been valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as alleviating respiratory issues, reducing inflammation, and potentially supporting immune function. Beyeria viscosa var. oblongifolia can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, with some herbalists recommending it be used in combination with other plants to enhance its medicinal effects.
